etymology.description: 之(之) (zhī, "of") is a classical possessive particle often used in literary Chinese to indicate possession or association, acting like the English word 'of'. 意(意) (yì, "intention; meaning") represents the concept of intent or meaning.
etymology.interpretation: 之(之) (zhī, "of") connects a modifier to a core noun, in this case linking to 意(意) (yì, "intention"), which conveys the notion of a thought or plan. Together, 之意 (zhī yì) refers specifically to the intention or meaning of something, capturing the abstract notion that lies behind words or actions.

examples.chinese: 他的话中有讽刺之意(他的話中有諷刺之意)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de huà zhōng yǒu fěngcì zhī yì.
examples.english: His words contain an intention of sarcasm.

examples.chinese: 老师的话里隐藏着鼓励之意(老師的話裡隱藏著鼓勵之意)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī de huà lǐ yǐncángzhe gǔlì zhī yì.
examples.english: The teacher's words conceal an intention of encouragement.

examples.chinese: 他的礼物表达了感谢之意(他的禮物表達了感謝之意)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de lǐwù biǎodále gǎnxiè zhī yì.
examples.english: His gift expressed an intention of gratitude.
